description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its length. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between the rough, speckled stone and dark recessed channels. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a consistent dark coloration, suggesting a dense stone material. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and resistance to wear. The surface texture and groove pattern indicate it may be used for anti-slip or drainage purposes.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, alternating between rough stone and dark channels. The sides are smooth and solid, emphasizing durability.
description_view_0: Front view shows the block's flat face with horizontal grooves visible on the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the deep, vertical grooves running the full length of the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with horizontal grooves visible and a solid, dark side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the vertical grooves, emphasizing the striped pattern.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or anti-slip surface
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with pavement or adjacent blocks
